content:
🚀 jcorp-nomad | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a portable, self-contained offline media server for accessing and streaming various media types in environments without internet connectivity.
✨ Key Features: • Offline media streaming (movies, music, books, shows) • ESP32-S3 powered • Local Wi-Fi hotspot creation • Browser-based interface • Multiple simultaneous user streams
📖 Summary: Jcorp Nomad is an open-source, offline media server built on an ESP32-S3, designed to stream movies, music, books, and shows without an internet connection. It creates a local Wi-Fi hotspot and serves content through a browser interface, making it ideal for travel, remote locations, or situations without internet access.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 PolyUploader | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified and efficient way for users to upload files to a large number of file hosting services simultaneously, with added features for management and link security.
✨ Key Features: • Upload to 133 hosts at once • Link personal API keys • Detailed upload history with expiration status and delete options
📖 Summary: PolyUploader is an open-source JavaScript application that allows users to upload files to multiple remote hosting sites simultaneously. It supports linking personal API keys, managing upload history, creating profiles for automation, and offers features like link protection and bundling multiple host links into a single shareable link.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 ESP32-Paxcounter | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Real-time passenger flow metering and environmental data collection using low-cost ESP32 devices, with an emphasis on privacy and flexible data transmission.
✨ Key Features: • WiFi & Bluetooth signal detection for passenger flow metering • LoRaWAN enabled for long-range data transmission • Multi-sensor data collection (e.g., BME680, GPS)
📖 Summary: The ESP32-Paxcounter is a project that uses ESP32 microcontrollers to count people by detecting WiFi and Bluetooth signals from mobile devices. It also supports environmental sensors and offers various data transmission options like LoRaWAN and MQTT, with a focus on privacy by not storing MAC addresses.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 StableAnimator | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Generating realistic and identity-consistent animated videos of humans from static images and pose sequences.
✨ Key Features: • End-to-end ID-preserving video diffusion framework • Synthesizes high-quality videos without post-processing • Conditioned on reference image and sequence of poses • Global content-aware Face Encoder for identity consistency • Distribution-aware ID Adapter to prevent temporal interference
📖 Summary: StableAnimator is a novel end-to-end framework for identity-preserving human image animation using video diffusion models. It synthesizes high-quality videos directly from a reference image and a sequence of poses, without requiring any post-processing like face swapping or restoration.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 core | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a private, secure, and self-hostable home security camera system that prioritizes data privacy through end-to-end encryption and avoids reliance on third-party cloud services.
✨ Key Features: • End-to-end encrypted remote access • 5-minute setup • Open source • Fully reproducible releases • Local AI capabilities (implied by topics)
📖 Summary: Secluso is a privacy-focused, open-source home security camera system for Raspberry Pi. It offers end-to-end encrypted remote access to live video, alerts, and recordings, ensuring footage is not shared with cloud providers. The system emphasizes a quick 5-minute setup and reproducible builds for transparency and trust.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 NauEnginePublic | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Developing and building video games with a flexible and accessible game engine.
✨ Key Features: • Free and open-source • No limitations or royalties • Full source code available • Permissive for educational and commercial use • Community development encouraged
📖 Summary: Nau Engine is a free, open-source, and versatile game engine built with C++. It allows for unrestricted use, including commercial purposes, and encourages community contributions through its open-source nature and accessible source code.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
